summary: Updated the local-to-server engine plan with a real Agentic RL framing: Datalox is the tool-I/O evidence layer that produces pinned fixture worlds and grounded task/trajectory/transition/reward/completion exports, not a schema-first product.

agentTurn.user_prompt: so what's ur update framing, what we should do to our current repo, think about it as from a real people who does agentic rl, no imagination, then update to the html
agentTurn.assistant_summary: Updated docs/local-to-server-engine-plan.html so the repo plan now matches a real Agentic RL data workflow. The plan says Datalox is not a schema product; it is the tool-I/O evidence layer that gives rollout, eval, and data teams a pinned tool world plus grounded exports. It adds a Real Agentic RL Data Jobs section, clarifies ownership boundaries between replay bundles, fixture worlds, trajectories, transitions, completion frames, reward records, and splits, updates current status, adds an Agentic RL export phase, adds an export release gate, and changes the recommended next tasks to fixture worlds, grounded exports, and private fixture recording.

tool_calls.output_summary: Focused docs and identity gates passed: 2 files, 12 tests.

file_changes.diff_summary: Reframed the product sentence around verified fixture worlds and grounded trajectory/eval exports. Added real Agentic RL data jobs and non-overlapping ownership rules for replay bundles, fixture worlds, trajectories, transitions, completion frames, reward records, and splits. Updated current state, Phase 3 export plan, release gates, server registry wording, and recommended next tasks.

verification.command: PATH="/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:$PATH" npx vitest run tests/replayCanonicalDocs.test.ts tests/repoIdentity.test.ts && PATH="/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:$PATH" npm run check && git diff --check
verification.evidence: Focused docs and identity gates passed: 2 files, 12 tests. TypeScript no-emit check passed. git diff --check passed.
